Motta provides Quarterly Bookkeeping Review services to help businesses maintain accurate and reliable financial records. Our review process ensures your books are well-organized, compliant, and ready for tax reporting or financial decision-making.

Our Services Include:

✅ Transaction Review – We review general ledger transactions for accuracy, completeness, and proper categorization.
✅ Bank & Credit Card Reconciliations – We verify that bank and credit card statements match your accounting records.
✅ Financial Statement Analysis – We assess key financial reports, including the Balance Sheet, Profit & Loss Statement, and Cash Flow Statement, for inconsistencies or errors.
✅ Adjusting Entries – We identify and recommend necessary adjustments, such as reclassifications or accrual entries, to improve financial accuracy.
✅ Compliance Check – We ensure your financial records align with applicable accounting standards and IRS regulations.
✅ Quarterly Review Meeting – We discuss findings, address any concerns, and provide actionable insights for financial improvement.

With our expert oversight, you gain confidence in your financial data, allowing you to make informed business decisions and streamline year-end tax preparation.
Service Terms:
1. Scope of Services

  • We provide a quarterly review of bookkeeping records, financial statements, and reconciliations based on the data provided by the client.
  • This service does not include transaction entry, full bookkeeping services, payroll processing, or tax preparation unless separately agreed upon.
  • If bookkeeping errors or discrepancies are identified, we will recommend corrective actions but will not make direct changes unless separately engaged.

2. Client Responsibilities

  • The client must provide timely access to all relevant financial records, including bank and credit card statements, accounting software, and any supporting documentation.
  • The client is responsible for implementing recommended adjustments or engaging us for additional services if corrections are required.
  • If the client fails to provide necessary information on time, the review may be delayed or rescheduled.

3. Fees & Payment Terms

  • Our fees are based on the size and complexity of the client's books and the level of review required.
  • Payment is due before the completion of the quarterly review unless otherwise agreed in writing.
  • Late payments may result in service suspension and/or additional fees.

4. Review Timelines & Deadlines

  • Reviews are typically completed within [X] business days after all required documents are received.
  • A quarterly review meeting will be scheduled at a mutually agreed-upon time.

5. Confidentiality & Data Security

  • All client data is kept confidential and secured in accordance with professional and legal standards.
  • We do not share client financial information with third parties without explicit authorization.

6. Limitation of Liability

  • Our review services are based on the accuracy of the financial data provided by the client. We are not responsible for errors due to incomplete or incorrect records.
  • We do not guarantee that the review process will identify all bookkeeping errors or fraud.

7. Termination of Services

  • Either party may terminate this agreement with [X] days' written notice.
  • If terminated after work has begun, the client will be billed for services rendered up to the termination date.

Service Description:
Our Individual 1040 Tax Preparation Service is designed to simplify the process of filing your federal and state income tax returns. We provide meticulous attention to detail to ensure compliance with all applicable tax laws while identifying opportunities to optimize your tax position. Whether your tax situation is straightforward or involves more complex factors, we deliver personalized and professional service to meet your needs. To assist you in your legal obligation to file an annual tax return. This includes:

  • Preparation of your Individual Tax Return
  • Electronic filing of federal tax return to taxing authorities

Scope of Services

  1. Federal Tax Return Preparation
    • Preparation and filing of your federal individual income tax return (Form 1040).
    • Reporting of income from wages, interest, dividends, retirement accounts, and other common sources.
  2. State Tax Return Preparation
    • Preparation and filing of one state income tax return with an add on fee for each state.
  3. Deduction and Credit Maximization
    • Review and application of eligible deductions and credits, including but not limited to the standard or itemized deduction, education credits, child-related credits, and retirement savings contributions.
  4. Tax Compliance and Accuracy
    • Ensuring compliance with federal and state tax laws, including proper reporting of income, deductions, and credits.
  5. Client Support
    • Up to one hour of consultation to address questions, clarify tax strategies, or provide tax planning insights.

Exclusions

  • This service does not include filing of amended returns, representation during IRS or state audits, or preparation of specialized forms (e.g., Schedule C, D, E, F) unless explicitly agreed upon in a separate engagement.

Your Commitment

You are responsible for providing all necessary tax documents, including Forms W-2, 1099, 1098, prior-year tax returns, and other applicable records. Complete and accurate submission of documents ensures timely and precise preparation of your return.
Service Terms:
1. Scope of Services
    • This engagement includes preparation and filing of your federal Form 1040 as outlined above.
    • Additional services such as preparation of amended returns, audit representation, or forms for more complex income (e.g., rental properties, investments, or self-employment) are not included unless agreed upon in advance.
  2. Client Responsibilities
    • You agree to provide all required tax documents by the requested deadlines to avoid delays in filing. Missing or incomplete documentation may require an extension to be filed.
  3. Confidentiality and Data Security
    • All information provided will be handled with the utmost confidentiality and protected by advanced data security measures.
  4. Timing and Deadlines
    • Documents received after March 15th may result in an automatic extension filing to ensure accurate preparation of your return.
  5. Termination of Services
    • Either party may terminate this agreement in writing prior to the completion of services. A prorated fee will be charged for work performed up to the point of termination.

Service Name: TAX | IRS Representation | Correspondence Audit Defense
Service Description:
Service Description

This service is designed for IRS correspondence audits involving multiple items, higher scrutiny areas (e.g., credits, filing status, Schedule C activity), or prior year audit exposure.

Motta Financial will:

  • Perform a detailed review of the IRS notice and related tax return(s)
  • Analyze prior year filings where relevant to identify recurring audit risks
  • Provide a comprehensive document request and audit support checklist
  • Conduct an internal “audit-style” review of the tax return and supporting documentation
  • Identify strengths, weaknesses, and potential exposure areas
  • Develop a response strategy (defend positions or recommend adjustments)
  • Prepare and submit a complete, organized audit response package to the IRS
  • Respond to one (1) additional round of IRS follow-up correspondence, if needed

This service is appropriate for moderate complexity audits requiring analysis, documentation reconstruction, and strategic response.
Service Terms:
• This engagement includes up to two (2) IRS correspondence responses (initial + one follow-up).
  • Scope is limited to items identified in the original IRS notice. New issues raised by the IRS may require an expanded engagement.
  • Client agrees to provide all requested documentation, including prior year audit results if applicable.
  • Motta Financial will rely on client-provided documentation and representations and does not guarantee audit outcomes.
  • If positions cannot be substantiated, Motta Financial may recommend amended filings or alternative resolution strategies, which are not included in this scope.
  • This service does not include IRS appeals, collections work, amended tax return preparation, or representation before tax court.
  • Additional work outside the defined scope will be billed separately or require a service upgrade.
  • Timely communication and document submission by the client is critical to meeting IRS deadlines.

Meeting to discuss current tax situation and potential year-end tax moves. We will also discuss your business operations and look for areas of improvement.

State Tax Return Preparation

1. Scope of Service

Motta Financial will prepare and file your individual state tax return(s) based on the information provided by you. Services include:

  • Review of income, deductions, and credits applicable to state tax regulations.
  • Preparation of the state tax return, ensuring compliance with all relevant laws and regulations.
  • Filing of the completed state return electronically or by mail (where applicable).

The client is responsible for providing all necessary documentation for the preparation of the state return, including but not limited to:

Failure to provide accurate and complete documentation may delay filing or result in additional fees.

3. Pricing

State tax return preparation is billed as an additional service to the federal return. Fees will be communicated upfront and may vary based on:

  • The complexity of the state tax laws or filing requirements.

Payment must be made in full before filing the state return.

4. Filing Deadlines

The client is responsible for providing all required documents to Motta Financial by the deadlines communicated.

  • Motta Financial will make reasonable efforts to complete and file the state return before the applicable deadline, provided all documentation is submitted on time.
  • If an extension is required, additional fees may apply.

5. Responsibility for Tax Liability

The client is solely responsible for:

  • Payment of any state tax due, including penalties and interest for late payments.
  • Ensuring the accuracy of all information provided for the preparation of the return.

Motta Financial is not responsible for penalties or interest resulting from incomplete, inaccurate, or late information provided by the client.

6. Audit Protection (Optional)

Clients may elect to purchase MottaGuard Audit Protection, which includes representation in the event of an audit or inquiry related to the state tax return prepared by Motta Financial. This service is optional and available for an additional fee.

7. Confidentiality

Motta Financial is committed to safeguarding your personal and financial information. All data provided will be treated as confidential and used solely for the purpose of preparing and filing your state tax return.

8. Amendments and Corrections

  • If an amendment to a state return is required due to an error by Motta Financial, it will be corrected at no additional charge.
  • Amendments due to client-provided omissions or changes after filing will incur additional fees.

9. Termination of Services

Either party may terminate this agreement at any time. If terminated by the client after preparation has begun, fees for services rendered will be due. No refunds will be issued for completed work.

10. Dispute Resolution

Any disputes arising from these terms will first be addressed through mediation. If unresolved, disputes may proceed to binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

11. Acceptance of Terms

By engaging Motta Financial for state tax return preparation, you acknowledge and accept these terms. For questions or additional information, contact us at info@mottafinancial.com or call (702) 514-6055.

Our Tax Preparation Service for Individuals with a Schedule D is tailored to address the complexities of reporting capital gains and losses from investments, including stocks, bonds, mutual funds, cryptocurrency, and other taxable transactions. We ensure accurate reporting and compliance with federal and state tax laws while helping you strategically manage your tax liability. Services include:

  • Determining short-term vs long-term holding periods
  • Comprehensive review of investment sales documentation (e.g., 1099-B).
  • Application of loss carryforwards to offset taxable gains.
  • Calculation of net capital gain or loss.
  • Preparing Schedule D with accurate totals
  • Attaching completed Schedule D to Form 1040

Exclusions

Your Commitment

Missing or incomplete documentation may delay the preparation of your return or require filing an extension.

Service Name: Tax | Prep (FBAR): Report of Foreign Bank and Financial Accounts
Service Description:
Managing foreign bank account reporting can be complex, but at Motta Financial, we simplify the process. Our FBAR preparation and filing service ensures compliance with the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) requirements, giving you peace of mind and protecting you from potential penalties. We'll prepare and file the Report of Foreign Bank and Financial Accounts (FBAR) on your behalf to report financial interests in or authority over foreign accounts.

What's Included:

  • Comprehensive review of your foreign financial account details to ensure accuracy.
  • Preparation and filing of the FinCEN Form 114 (FBAR) by the annual June 30 deadline.
  • Analysis of filing thresholds to confirm FBAR requirements based on your aggregate account values.
  • Ongoing guidance to address queries related to foreign account reporting.

Our expertise ensures your FBAR is prepared accurately and in compliance with federal regulations. Add this service to your tax preparation package for seamless, worry-free compliance.
Service Terms:
Service Terms

  • Scope of Service: Motta Financial will assist in the preparation and filing of the FinCEN Form 114 (FBAR) based on the information provided by the client. The service includes verifying account balances and ensuring compliance with applicable reporting thresholds.
  • Client Responsibilities: Clients are responsible for providing all necessary documentation, including foreign account statements, account holder details, and tax identification numbers, by the agreed-upon deadlines. Late submission may impact the ability to file timely.
  • Filing Deadline: The FBAR is due annually on or before June 30. It is the client's responsibility to provide all required documentation no later than May 15 to ensure timely filing.
  • Fee Structure: The FBAR preparation and filing service is offered as an add-on to your tax preparation package. The standard fee is $250 per filing, subject to adjustment for complexity.
  • Compliance Guarantee: While Motta Financial will make every effort to ensure compliance, ultimate responsibility for accurate and timely reporting remains with the client. Any penalties arising from inaccurate or incomplete information provided by the client will not be the liability of Motta Financial.
  • Payment Terms: Payment for FBAR preparation services will be due upon acceptance of the service proposal. Invoices will be sent via a secure payment link.

Authorization & Approval

By adding the FBAR Preparation & Filing Add-On, you acknowledge the terms outlined above and agree to provide the required documentation by the specified deadlines.

Motta's Pre-409A Valuation Analysis service will provide an estimated valuation of your business to support planning, option grant strategy discussions, and to streamline a future “formal” 409A if/when needed. We will perform an investor graade business valuation to determine your company's fair market value. This includes analyzing financials, metrics, industry trends and outlook to reach a supportable value conclusion. The detailed valuation report will outline the methodologies used, assumptions made, sensitivity analysis, and value conclusion to help substantiate the valuation.

Important positioning (recommended language):
This engagement provides an indicative / planning valuation and supporting documentation. A company seeking 409A “safe harbor” protection generally relies on a valuation that meets the regulatory reasonableness framework and often uses an independent appraisal approach. Treasury regulations describe reasonable valuation factors and safe-harbor-style presumptions for private company equity valuations.

Valuation Approach (typical for Pre-409A)

  • Income Approach: DCF based on the 5-year forecast + terminal value assumptions
  • Market Approach: sanity checks using relevant multiples and/or guideline public comps (when data is available)
  • Adjustments / discounts: supportable discussion of marketability/illiquidity and company-specific risk (planning-grade; final 409A providers may apply more formal methodologies)

Deliverables — Pre-409A

  1. Pre-409A Valuation Memo including:
    • Purpose and valuation date
    • Standard of value (FMV framework discussion)
    • Methods used (DCF + market approach checks)
    • Key assumptions and sensitivities (WACC, terminal growth, margins, growth)
    • Valuation conclusion (enterprise and equity value; and, if requested, a preliminary common stock indication subject to cap table detail and preference terms)
  2. Assumptions & Inputs Appendix (written + tabular)
  3. Data Room Checklist / “409A Readiness” Packet (organizes the documents a formal 409A firm typically requests)

A CPA Verification Letter is a formal document prepared by a Certified Public Accountant (CPA) to confirm specific financial or business details of an individual or organization. This letter is often requested by lenders or financial institutions as part of the loan application process, especially for self-employed individuals or small business owners. It provides independent verification of income, employment, or other financial information, helping lenders assess creditworthiness and compliance with their requirements.

Key Features of the CPA Verification Letter Service:

  1. Income Verification: Confirms an individual's or business's reported income for a specific period, often based on tax returns or financial statements.
  2. Employment Verification: Confirms an individual's role or ownership in a business.
  3. Business Financial Health: Provides details about the financial status of a business, including revenue, expenses, and profitability.
  4. Loan-Specific Details: Tailored letters to meet specific requirements of the lender, such as verifying the borrower's ability to repay a loan.

Common Uses of a CPA Verification Letter:

  • Mortgage or loan applications.
  • Lines of credit applications.
  • SBA (Small Business Administration) loans.
  • Rental applications (verifying income for landlords).
  • Financial certifications or declarations for business transactions.

What the CPA Verification Letter Includes:

  • CPA's name, firm, contact information, and license number.
  • Borrower's financial or income information (based on what is being verified).
  • Confirmation of how the information was verified (e.g., tax filings, business records).
  • Any disclaimers to limit the scope of the CPA's responsibility, as this is not an audit or formal opinion.

Typical Turnaround Time: 1–3 business days, depending on the complexity of the request.
Service Terms:
1. Scope of Service:
    • The CPA Verification Letter will only confirm financial information based on existing documentation provided by the client (e.g., tax returns, financial statements, or bank records).
    • This service does not involve independent audits, reviews, or the creation of financial documents.
  2. Client Responsibilities:
    • The client must provide accurate and complete financial records and information necessary for the CPA to prepare the verification letter.
    • It is the client's responsibility to ensure that the CPA Verification Letter satisfies the lender's specific requirements (e.g., format, content, or delivery method).
  3. Limitations and Disclaimers:
    • The letter is issued solely for the purpose requested by the client (e.g., a loan application) and is not intended to be relied upon by any third party for other purposes.
    • The CPA does not assume any liability for decisions made by lenders or third parties based on the verification letter.
  4. Fees and Payment:
    • The fee for a CPA Verification Letter is typically $150 to $500, depending on the complexity of the verification and the amount of work involved. A non-refundable deposit may be required upon engagement.
    • Rush service (within 24 hours) may incur an additional fee of $50 to $100.
  5. Confidentiality:
    • All client information provided to the CPA will remain confidential and be handled in compliance with applicable federal and state privacy laws.
  6. Delivery:
    • The verification letter will be delivered electronically (via email) or as a physical document, based on the client's preference.
  7. Engagement Agreement:
    • Before work begins, the client must sign an engagement letter outlining the scope of the verification, fees, and other terms of service.

Service Description:
With Motta's Financial Statement Forecasting service, we will work with you to build an investor-/board-ready, driver-based forecast model projecting Income Statement, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow for a 3–5 year forecast, with clear written assumptions and a narrative package that explains why the forecast is reasonable. We research and analyze historical financials, industry trends, benchmarks, and business assumptions to create a 3–5 year forecast that aligns with lender expectations.

Objective
 

Phase 1 — Discovery & Data Intake

  • Kickoff meeting to confirm:
    • Business model and revenue engines
    • Operating cost structure
    • Hiring plan and compensation structure
    • Working capital behavior (AR/AP timing), capex needs, debt/equity plans
  • Data request + intake (typical):
    • Historical financials (GL / TB / monthly P&L, BS)
    • Revenue detail (pipeline, cohorts, pricing, unit metrics)
    • Payroll roster & planned hires
    • Debt schedules, investor docs (if any), cap table summary (high level)
    • Key contracts, commission plans, insurance / vendor agreements (as applicable)

Phase 2 — Forecast Build (Model Construction)

  • Build a driver-based model with:
    • Central assumptions table (growth rates, rates, unit economics)
    • Revenue build (volume × price × conversion/attach, etc.)
    • Expense build (COGS + opex with fixed/variable behavior)
    • Integrated 3-statement outputs (P&L, BS, CF)
    • KPI dashboard (growth, margins, runway, cash needs, etc.)
    • Scenario functionality (Base / Upside / Downside) and sensitivity where appropriate

Phase 3 — Review, Refinement, and Close

  • Iterative working sessions to reconcile:
    • Outputs to management expectations
    • Variance checks vs. historical trends and unit metrics
    • Cash runway and funding needs
  • Finalization and delivery package

Deliverables — Forecast

  1. Excel Forecast Model (integrated 3 statements, assumptions, dashboards, scenarios)
  2. Forecast Assumptions Memo (written narrative of key drivers, logic, and rationale)
  3. Forecast Summary (PDF) for sharing with stakeholders (high-level outputs + key assumptions)
  4. Support Schedules / Supplements (as applicable): hiring schedule, capex, AR/AP timing, debt roll-forward, etc.
Service Terms:
1. Parties and Engagement

These Service Terms (“Terms”) govern Motta Financial’s provision of (i) a Five-Year Financial Statement Forecast and/or (ii) a Pre-409A Valuation Package (collectively, the “Services”).

2. Scope of Services

Motta will perform the Services described in the Service Description above, limited to the agreed deliverables. Any services not expressly included (e.g., audit, review, compilation, tax return preparation, legal services, fairness opinions, or a formal 409A safe-harbor appraisal) are excluded unless added by written change order.

3. Client Responsibilities

Client will:

  • Provide accurate, complete, and timely information and documents.
  • Make management available for interviews and review meetings.
  • Review drafts within agreed time windows and provide consolidated feedback.

Motta may rely on information provided by Client without independent verification, unless otherwise agreed.

4. Assumptions, Forward-Looking Information, and No Guarantee

Forecasts/projections are based on assumptions and forward-looking judgments. Actual results may differ—possibly materially—from projected results due to internal and external factors beyond Motta’s control.

5. Valuation-Specific Use and Reliance Limitations

  • The Pre-409A valuation is intended for planning and internal decision support unless expressly stated otherwise in the engagement letter.
  • If Client intends to use outputs to set equity compensation strike prices for compliance purposes, Client is responsible for ensuring the valuation approach and documentation meets applicable tax and regulatory requirements; Treasury regulations describe reasonable valuation considerations and presumptions for private stock valuations.
  • No third party may rely on Motta’s work without Motta’s prior written consent.

6. Deliverable Format and Acceptance

Deliverables may include Excel workbooks, PDFs, and written memos/appendices. Deliverables are deemed accepted when delivered unless Client provides written notice of material nonconformity within 10 business days.

7. Revisions and Change Orders

Fees include up to:

  • Two (2) consolidated revision rounds on the forecast model after initial draft delivery, and
  • One (1) consolidated revision round on the Pre-409A memo after draft delivery.

Additional revisions, new scenarios, structural changes, or scope expansion are billed via change order.

8. Fees, Billing, and Payment

  • Fees are fixed-fee or fixed-fee + hourly (as quoted).
  • Typical payment schedule (customize per engagement):
    • 50% deposit at kickoff
    • 40% upon delivery of draft forecast + draft pre-409A memo
    • 10% upon delivery of final package
  • Past-due amounts may pause work until current.

9. Confidentiality and Data Security

Motta will maintain confidentiality of Client materials and use secure exchange methods for document transfer. Client authorizes Motta to use necessary subcontractors or platforms under confidentiality obligations.

10. Workpapers and Intellectual Property

Motta retains ownership of templates, tools, and workpapers. Client receives a non-transferable license to use final deliverables internally for the intended purpose.

11. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Motta’s aggregate liability related to the Services is limited to the fees paid for the specific Services giving rise to the claim. Motta will not be liable for consequential, incidental, or punitive damages.

12. Termination

Either party may terminate with written notice. Client remains responsible for fees incurred through termination date, including non-cancelable third-party costs (if any).

13. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law

Add your preferred venue/state governing law language (e.g., Nevada/Massachusetts—choose consistent with the client contract).

All financial information shared with us is kept strictly confidential and will not be disclosed without client consent.

Service Name: Tax | IRS Support | Request for Abatement
Service Description:
This service includes the preparation and submission of a formal request for IRS penalty abatement due to reasonable cause or first-time penalty relief (FTA). Our team will assess the penalties applied, determine the best approach for reduction, and advocate on your behalf with the IRS to minimize your tax liability.

Scope of Service:
✔ Penalty Review & Analysis: Assess penalties for failure-to-file, failure-to-pay, and estimated tax underpayments.

✔ Penalty Abatement Request Preparation: Draft and submit a formal abatement request (IRS Form 843 or written appeal).
✔ IRS Follow-Up & Negotiation: Monitor IRS responses and provide additional documentation if requested.
✔ Resolution Guidance: Provide next steps based on IRS determination, including payment plans if necessary.

🚀 Expected Outcome: Successful removal or reduction of penalties, potentially lowering your overall tax liability.

⏳ Estimated Timeline: 4 to 12 weeks, depending on IRS processing times.

Service Description:

Whether you're just starting out or need a reset for your accounting processes, we provide comprehensive solutions tailored to your unique needs. This service includes:

  • Customized Chart of Accounts Creation: We'll design and implement a chart of accounts specific to your business, ensuring a clear and organized financial structure that aligns with industry standards and your operational needs.
  • Year-to-Date Catch-Up and Clean-Up: For businesses needing to reconcile past transactions, we'll clean up and catch up your books year-to-date, ensuring accuracy and compliance.
  • Platform Integration: We'll seamlessly connect your business platforms—such as POS systems, payroll providers, and CRM tools—to your accounting software, enhancing efficiency and reducing manual input.
  • General Ledger Optimization: Our team will streamline your general ledger by automating recurring transactions, setting up rules, and reducing redundancies, creating a time-saving and scalable accounting system.
  • Tailored Monthly Reporting Package: Gain clarity and insights with a customized monthly reporting package. We'll provide key performance indicators, financial statements, and actionable insights, designed to meet the specific needs of your business.

With our Bookkeeping Set-Up and Optimization service, you'll not only achieve accurate and compliant financial records but also gain valuable insights and efficiencies that position your business for growth. Let us take care of the details so you can focus on what you do best.
Service Terms:
• Scope of Work:
    This service includes:
    • Creation and implementation of a customized chart of accounts.
    • Year-to-date reconciliation and clean-up of existing financial records.
    • Integration of specified business platforms into the accounting system.
    • Optimization of the general ledger for automated transaction processing.
    • Development and delivery of a tailored monthly reporting package.
      Any additional services requested outside this scope may incur additional charges.
  • Client Responsibilities:
    • Timely provision of accurate and complete financial records, including transaction details, receipts, and access to relevant systems.
    • Access to necessary third-party platforms, such as POS, payroll, and CRM systems, to facilitate integration.
    • Prompt response to inquiries related to financial records and business operations.
  • Timeline and Deliverables:
    • The setup and optimization process will be completed within a mutually agreed timeline, contingent on the timely submission of all required documents and access credentials by the client.
    • The tailored monthly reporting package will be delivered at the end of each reporting period (e.g., monthly, quarterly) as specified in the agreement.
  • Confidentiality:
    • All client financial data and business information will be handled with the utmost confidentiality and used solely for the purpose of providing the agreed-upon services.
  • Payment Terms:
    • A non-refundable deposit is required to initiate services.
    • The remaining balance is due upon completion of the bookkeeping set-up and optimization process unless otherwise specified in the agreement.
    • Monthly reporting services will be billed at an agreed-upon recurring fee.
  • Accuracy Disclaimer:
    • Motta Financial will rely on the information provided by the client for the completion of this service. Errors or omissions in the information provided may affect the accuracy and reliability of deliverables. Motta Financial is not liable for issues arising from incomplete or incorrect client data.
  • Platform Compatibility:
    • Integration of business platforms is limited to those compatible with the agreed-upon accounting software. Additional fees may apply for integrating non-standard systems or platforms requiring custom solutions.
  • Termination of Services:
    • Either party may terminate the service agreement with written notice. In the event of termination, the client is responsible for payment of all work completed up to the termination date.
  • Liability:
    • Motta Financial is not responsible for compliance issues or penalties that arise due to errors or omissions in prior accounting records outside the scope of this service.
  • Amendments:
    • Changes to the scope of services, deliverables, or timelines must be agreed upon in writing by both parties and may result in adjustments to fees or deadlines.

Service Description:
Our C Corporation Tax Preparation service ensures your business meets its federal and state tax filing obligations accurately and efficiently. We prepare and file Form 1120, including all necessary schedules, credits, and deductions, while optimizing tax positions in compliance with the latest IRS and state regulations. Our goal is to help your corporation minimize tax liability, maximize compliance, and avoid unnecessary penalties.

What's Included:

✅ Preparation and filing of Form 1120 (U.S. Corporation Income Tax Return)
✅ State corporate tax return preparation (as applicable)
✅ Standard business deductions & credits review
✅ Depreciation & fixed asset reporting (Section 179, Bonus Depreciation, etc.)
✅ Book-to-tax reconciliation (if applicable)
✅ Estimated tax payment calculations (if needed)
✅ E-filing & confirmation of acceptance

What's Not Included (But Available as Add-ons):

➕ Multi-state tax returns (additional per state)
➕ Nexus analysis & state tax compliance review
➕ IRS/State audit representation
➕ Tax planning & strategy sessions
Service Terms:
Motta agrees to prepare and file the Client's C Corporation federal tax return (Form 1120) and applicable state returns based on information provided by the Client. Additional services outside this scope may require a separate engagement agreement.

Client Responsibilities

  • Provide accurate and complete financial records (income statement, balance sheet, trial balance, etc.).
  • Respond to requests for additional information in a timely manner to avoid delays.
  • Ensure prior-year tax returns are available (if applicable).

Exclusions

This engagement does not include bookkeeping, payroll services, or financial statement preparation. Additionally, representation before the IRS or state agencies is not included but can be provided under a separate engagement.

Service Description:
At Motta, we specialize in guiding businesses through complex legal entity restructurings, ensuring compliance with federal and state tax laws while optimizing for tax efficiency and liability protection. Our entity restructuring services cover a range of strategic business changes, including:

  • State Re-Domestication: Moving an entity's legal home from one state to another while maintaining operations.
  • Entity Type Conversion: Changing your business structure (e.g., from an LLC to an S corporation) to better align with tax and operational goals.
  • Dissolution & Business Wind-Down: Properly closing a legal entity to ensure compliance with state and federal regulations, avoiding unnecessary penalties.
  • Mergers & Consolidations: Combining entities for operational or tax benefits.
  • Multi-State Compliance & Registrations: Ensuring your business meets filing and tax requirements in new jurisdictions.

We handle entity restructuring from start to finish, including:
✔ Reviewing tax and legal implications of the change
✔ Preparing and filing necessary state and federal documents
✔ Updating tax registrations, EINs, and business licenses
✔ Coordinating with legal counsel for complex transitions

Whether you're looking to optimize your tax position, simplify compliance, or adapt to new business needs, Motta provides the expertise to execute these changes smoothly.
Service Terms:
1. Scope of Services
    • Motta provides advisory and compliance support for restructuring legal entities but does not provide legal services. We recommend consulting with an attorney for legal contract reviews.
    • Services include entity analysis, state filings, IRS notifications, and coordination with legal professionals as needed.
  2. Pricing
    • State Re-Domestication: $1,500 – $3,500 (varies by state filing fees and complexity)
    • Entity Type Conversion: $1,000 – $2,500
    • Dissolution & Wind-Down: $1,200 – $2,500
    • Multi-State Compliance & Registration: $750 – $2,000 per state
    • Customized Consulting & Complex Restructuring: Hourly rate of $350 (minimum 5 hours) or fixed fee based on project scope
  3. Filing Fees & Third-Party Costs
    • State fees for entity conversion, dissolution, and re-domestication vary and are billed separately.
    • Additional fees may apply for expedited filings or tax clearance certificates.
  4. Client Responsibilities
    • Provide accurate and timely business information necessary for filings.
    • Engage legal counsel as needed for contract reviews or liability concerns.
  5. Turnaround Time
    • Standard processing takes 3–6 weeks, depending on state agencies. Expedited services may be available for an additional fee.
  6. Disclaimers
    • Motta does not provide legal advice. Our services focus on tax implications and compliance.
    • State and federal tax laws change frequently. Clients should periodically review their entity structure with a tax professional.

Service Description: Outsourced

Our Corporate Tax Provision (ASC 740) Preparation service provides a comprehensive calculation and documentation of your company's income tax provision in accordance with Accounting Standards Codification (ASC) 740. We ensure compliance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) while minimizing tax risks and identifying potential opportunities.

This service is ideal for businesses required to prepare financial statement tax disclosures, including public and private companies that must comply with ASC 740 reporting requirements. Whether you need a full provision calculation or just assistance with deferred tax balances, our experienced tax professionals provide accurate and efficient support.

Key Deliverables:
✅ Current and Deferred Tax Calculation – Preparation of your company's annual tax provision, including reconciliation of book-to-tax differences.
✅ Deferred Tax Asset & Liability Analysis – Identifying and tracking temporary differences.
✅ Effective Tax Rate (ETR) Computation – Ensuring accuracy in your financial reporting.
✅ Uncertain Tax Positions (UTP) Analysis – Evaluating tax positions per FIN 48.
✅ Tax Footnote Disclosure Preparation – Assisting in drafting required financial statement disclosures.
✅ Audit Support – Providing documentation and explanations for external auditors.
Service Terms:
1. Scope of Work:
    • Our team will prepare your company's tax provision in accordance with ASC 740 for the specified tax year.
    • The service includes calculations, schedules, and supporting documentation for financial statement disclosures.
    • Assistance with auditor inquiries is included, but complex audit support beyond routine questions may incur additional fees.
    • This engagement does not include the preparation or filing of federal or state income tax returns unless separately agreed upon.
  2. Client Responsibilities:
    • Timely provision of financial statements, trial balances, tax return data, and any other necessary documents.
    • Cooperation with our team to clarify business transactions that may impact tax calculations.
  3. Pricing & Payment Terms:
    • Pricing is based on the company's size, complexity, and whether it is a public or private entity.
    • A 50% retainer is required upfront, with the remaining balance due upon completion.
    • Additional fees may apply if the scope expands beyond the original agreement.
  4. Revisions & Adjustments:
    • Up to two rounds of revisions are included. Additional revisions will be billed at an hourly rate.
    • If there are material changes in financials or tax positions after initial calculations, additional fees may apply.
  5. Limitations of Service:
    • We do not provide legal opinions or guarantee tax positions.
    • While we aim for accuracy, final tax positions should be reviewed with your external auditors and internal finance team.
  6. Confidentiality & Data Security:
    • All client data will be handled with strict confidentiality.
    • Secure portals will be used for document exchange to protect sensitive information.
  7. Additional Fees:
    1. Expedited turnaround (<2 weeks): 25% premium.
    2. Complex M&A or restructuring analysis: Billed separately.
    3. Uncertain tax position (FIN 48) analysis: Custom pricing based on complexity.
    4. Extended audit support beyond routine questions: Hourly billing at $250–$500/hr.

Service Description:
Our R&D Tax Credit Study helps businesses identify and maximize tax savings through the federal and state Research & Development (R&D) Tax Credit. This credit is available to companies engaged in activities that improve products, processes, software, or technology—regardless of industry.

Through a comprehensive analysis of qualifying expenses, we ensure compliance with IRS guidelines while optimizing your tax credit claim. Our team of tax professionals will work closely with your company to document eligible activities and expenses, minimizing risk and maximizing your benefit.

Key Deliverables:
✅ Eligibility Assessment – Identifying qualifying R&D activities under IRS Section 41.
✅ Qualified Research Expense (QRE) Calculation – Analysis of wages, supplies, and contract research costs.
✅ Tax Credit Computation – Calculation of federal and applicable state R&D credits.
✅ Substantiation & Documentation – Detailed reporting to support IRS and state compliance.
✅ Amended Returns (if applicable) – Assistance with retroactive claims for prior years.
✅ Audit Defense Support – If needed, we provide documentation and representation in the event of an IRS or state audit.
Service Terms:
1. Scope of Work:
    • We will conduct a thorough review of your company's R&D activities and expenses for the tax year(s) under analysis.
    • The service includes identification, documentation, and computation of eligible R&D tax credits.
    • If applicable, we will assist with amended return filings for prior-year claims.
    • Audit defense is not included but can be provided separately if required.
  2. Client Responsibilities:
    • Providing financial records, payroll data, project documentation, and other relevant materials.
    • Designating a key point of contact (finance, engineering, or R&D personnel) for interviews and data requests.
    • Ensuring timely submission of required documents to prevent delays.
  3. Pricing & Payment Terms:
    • Pricing is based on the size and complexity of your R&D activities. (See Typical Structure & Pricing Model below.)
    • A 50% deposit is required upfront, with the remaining balance due upon completion of the study.
    • Contingency-based pricing (e.g., % of credit secured) is available upon request but may be subject to legal restrictions.
  4. Revisions & Adjustments:
    • Up to two rounds of revisions are included. Additional revisions will be billed at an hourly rate.
    • If new eligible expenses are identified after initial calculations, additional fees may apply for recalculations.
  5. Limitations of Service:
    • We do not provide legal opinions on R&D tax positions.
    • While we aim for accuracy and compliance, final credit claims are subject to IRS/state review.
  6. Confidentiality & Data Security:
    • All client data will be handled with strict confidentiality.
    • Secure portals will be used for document exchange to protect sensitive information.
  7. Additional Fees:
    1. Expedited turnaround (<4 weeks): +25% premium.
    2. Multi-year study (claiming multiple tax years): Custom pricing.
    3. Audit support (if required): Hourly rate of $250–$500/hr.
  8. Contingency Pricing Option (if allowed in your jurisdiction):
    1. 10–20% of the credit secured, subject to a minimum fee.
    2. Hybrid models (lower fixed fee + percentage of credit) available upon request.

Let us handle the heavy lifting of getting your business up and running with the right structure. Our S Corporation Entity Formation service includes everything you need to form your business and stay compliant from day one. We'll guide you through the entire process — from state registration to federal filings — with white-glove support and expert advice at every step.

This service includes:

  • Entity Formation & State Registration (Secretary of State filing in your chosen state)
  • S Corporation Election (IRS Form 2553 filing)
  • EIN Application (Employer Identification Number through the IRS)
  • Professional Operating Agreement prepared by a partnering business attorney
  • Registered Agent Coordination (if applicable)

By the end of this process, your business will be properly structured as an S Corporation, legally compliant, and ready to operate — with fewer headaches and no "did-I-miss-anything?" stress.
Service Terms:
• Scope of Work: This engagement covers a one-time formation service for a single-member or multi-member S Corporation. This includes document preparation, filings, coordination with a licensed attorney for a custom Operating Agreement, and obtaining an EIN from the IRS.
  • Jurisdiction: Services are limited to formation in a single U.S. state. Additional state registrations (foreign qualification, sales tax permits, etc.) may be added for an additional fee.
  • Turnaround Time: Typical formation timelines range from 7–15 business days, depending on state processing times and responsiveness of client in providing required information.
  • Client Responsibilities: Client must provide timely responses to information requests and any personal identification documents needed for filings.
  • Exclusions: Ongoing legal advisory services, tax return preparation, and monthly bookkeeping are not included but may be quoted separately.
  • Legal Disclaimer: While we coordinate with legal professionals for certain deliverables (like the Operating Agreement), Motta is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice. We recommend all clients review formation documents with their own attorney.

Controllership Advisory at Motta Financial means you have a dedicated, high-level financial partner who oversees the accuracy, structure, and strategic use of your accounting information. This service goes beyond bookkeeping — it gives your company a financial "co-pilot" who ensures your numbers are reliable, your reporting is meaningful, and your leadership team has the information required to make confident business decisions.

What's Included

1. Monthly Financial Oversight & Review

  • Full review of financial statements, account classifications, and reconciliations
  • Verification that income, COGS, and job-related costs are coded correctly
  • Oversight of intercompany transactions and internal billings
  • Ensuring month-end closes follow consistent, documented procedures

2. Job Costing & WIP Review

  • Oversight of Work-in-Progress (WIP) reporting
  • Review of job profitability, budget compliance, and margin trends
  • Recommendations to improve estimating, cost tracking, and job-level reporting

3. Cashflow Monitoring

  • Monthly review of cash balances and cashburn trajectory
  • Analysis of owner distributions, payroll, and operating cash needs
  • Identification of short-term risks or liquidity concerns

4. Process & System Improvements

  • Review and improvement of financial processes (billing, AP/AR, reimbursements)
  • Business Platform ↔ QuickBooks workflow recommendations
  • Standardization of charts of accounts across all related entities

5. Strategic Advisory & Meetings

  • Monthly or quarterly advisory meetings
  • Financial interpretation and decision-support
  • Guidance on growth initiatives, entity structure, and scaling plans
  • Support for budgeting, forecasting, and capital planning

6. Collaboration With Your Accounting Team

  • We serve as the financial liaison between ownership, bookkeeping, and tax
  • Proactive identification of issues before they become problems
  • Coordination of tax planning, payroll, entity structuring, and compliance needs

The Goal

To give your company a consistent, strategic financial function — without the cost of hiring a full-time controller or CFO.
Service Terms:
1. Purpose of Engagement
The purpose of the Controllership Advisory service is to provide financial oversight, reporting review, process improvements, and strategic advisory support. This service is not a replacement for internal accounting staff or traditional bookkeeping, but enhances those functions by providing higher-level analysis and guidance.

2. Scope Limitations
This service does not include:

  • Daily data entry or transaction coding
  • AP/AR processing
  • Full-service payroll administration
  • Audit or assurance services
  • Legal or investment advice
    These services can be added through separate engagements if needed.

3. Deliverables
Deliverables generally include:

  • Monthly reviewed financial statements
  • WIP/job costing oversight
  • Monthly or quarterly advisory meetings
  • Process improvement recommendations
  • Ongoing communication and consulting
    Deliverables may vary based on workflow, complexity, and client responsiveness.

4. Client Responsibilities
To perform the services, the client agrees to:

  • Provide timely access to financial systems and records
  • Ensure all underlying bookkeeping is completed before review meetings
  • Communicate material changes in operations or structure
  • Respond to requests for clarification and documentation

5. Meetings & Communication
Controllership Advisory includes scheduled monthly or quarterly meetings (based on the service tier selected). Additional meetings or urgent requests may be billed separately.

6. Fee Structure
Fees are billed monthly at the rate specified in the engagement letter.
Additional out-of-scope work is billed at standard hourly rates and will be communicated before work begins.

7. Term & Cancellation
The engagement is month-to-month and may be terminated with 30 days' written notice by either party. Fees for services already performed are non-refundable.

8. Reliance on Information Provided
All recommendations and analysis rely on the accuracy of the underlying financial data provided by the client. Motta Financial is not responsible for errors in bookkeeping, third-party software limitations, or client-provided information.

9. Confidentiality
All financial and operational information shared during the engagement is kept confidential and used solely for the purposes of this engagement.

Service Description:
At Motta Financial, our Accounting Advisory team provides Monthly Account Reconciliation Services designed to ensure that your financial accounts are accurate, organized, and reliable. This service is not bookkeeping; rather, it is a higher-level monthly financial review focused on verifying accuracy, identifying discrepancies, and advising you proactively—so you can make informed decisions with confidence.

Our approach reflects Motta’s core values of clarity, proactive communication, and delivering “value-add” services that help clients operate efficiently and strategically.

What This Service Includes

Each month, Motta will perform the following:

1. Account Reconciliations

  • Bank account reconciliations
  • Credit card and line-of-credit reconciliations
  • Loan balance confirmations and reconciliations
  • Payment processor and merchant account reconciliations (e.g., Stripe, PayPal, Square)
  • Clearing account and suspense account reconciliations

2. Monthly Review Procedures

  • Review of account activity for completeness and accuracy
  • Identification of unusual, missing, or potentially erroneous transactions
  • High-level review of client-prepared bookkeeping (if applicable)
  • Variance checks against prior periods
  • Confirmation that balances agree to supporting documentation

3. Monthly Recommendations & Advisory Notes

  • Summary of reconciling items and proposed corrections
  • Recommendations to improve bookkeeping accuracy or efficiency
  • Highlighting operational, payroll, or accounting issues we identify
  • Suggestions for internal control improvements, when applicable
  • Forward-looking advisory insights where data trends warrant further discussion

4. Monthly Deliverables

  • Reconciliation workpapers
  • Monthly reconciliation completion summary
  • Advisory memo or bullet-point recommendations for the period
  • Notification through Karbon when monthly review is complete
Service Terms:
What This Service Does Not Include

(But can be added as separate services upon request.)

To preserve clarity in scope, the following items are not included in Monthly Account Reconciliation Services:

  • Day-to-day bookkeeping or transaction categorization
  • Accounts payable or accounts receivable processing
  • Payroll processing or payroll tax filings
  • Inventory accounting
  • Financial statement preparation (unless added separately)
  • Tax preparation or tax planning (offered separately by Motta Financial)
  • Catch-up or cleanup bookkeeping for prior periods
  • Audit, review, or compilation services under AICPA standards

If your books require cleanup before monthly reconciliations can begin, we will provide a separate quote for those services.

Client Responsibilities

To ensure accurate and timely reconciliations, the client agrees to:

  • Provide access to all relevant financial accounts (banking, credit cards, lenders, processors).
  • Ensure all accounting software and third-party platforms are functioning and accessible.
  • Upload requested documents promptly through Karbon checklists.
  • Maintain responsibility for day-to-day transaction categorization (unless separately contracted).
  • Notify Motta of material business changes that affect financial activity.

Failure to provide timely access or documentation may delay deliverables.

Timing & Workflow

Standard Monthly Workflow

  1. Data Availability Check
    Motta confirms all statements and data feeds are available for the month.
  2. Reconciliation Procedures
    Our Accounting Team performs reconciliations and review procedures.
  3. Exception Identification
    We list unresolved or unusual items requiring client input.
  4. Client Follow-Up
    Client responds to questions or uploads requested information.
  5. Finalization & Deliverables
    Motta finalizes reconciliations, provides a monthly summary, and issues recommendations.

Delivery Timing

Reconciliation work typically begins after the close of the month and receipt of all necessary statements. Standard delivery is within 10–20 business days after receipt of complete information.

Expedited delivery may be available upon request for an additional fee.

Fees & Billing Terms

  • Services are billed at a fixed monthly rate as outlined in your service quote.
  • The monthly fee covers reconciliations and advisory noted in this scope only.
  • Additional work outside of scope—e.g., bookkeeping fixes, cleanup, special reporting—is billed separately and will be quoted in advance.
  • Payment requests are issued securely through Motta’s online payment link.

Limitations & Disclaimers

  • Motta Financial does not provide assurance services (audit, review, or compilation) under this engagement.
  • Our monthly reconciliations are dependent on the accuracy and completeness of client-provided records.
  • Recommendations provided are based on information available and are advisory in nature.
  • Client retains ultimate responsibility for maintaining accurate books and records.

Term & Termination

  • This service continues month-to-month unless terminated by either party with 30 days’ written notice.
  • Fees for work already performed are non-refundable.
  • Motta may pause work if access to data or payment becomes delinquent.

Communication & Client Support

Motta’s team will communicate through:

  • Karbon (task requests, document uploads, progress updates)
  • Email
  • Scheduled advisory calls, as needed

We encourage ongoing communication—our goal is to support your financial clarity and overall business health.

Expedited CPA Verification Letter

Service Description

A CPA Verification Letter (sometimes referred to as a “CPA Comfort Letter” or “Accountant’s Letter”) is a written confirmation prepared by Motta Financial based on information contained in a client’s tax returns, financial records, and other documentation provided by the client. These letters are commonly requested by lenders, landlords, government agencies, or third parties for purposes such as income verification or financial status confirmation.

The Expedited CPA Verification Letter service prioritizes the preparation and delivery of a CPA Verification Letter outside of our standard workflow timelines. This service reallocates firm resources to address time-sensitive requests and ensure delivery by the requested expedited deadline, subject to information availability and professional standards.

This service includes:

  • Priority review of client-provided information
  • Preparation of a customized CPA Verification Letter consistent with professional standards
  • Accelerated internal review and approval
  • Delivery of the final letter via secure electronic means

Expedited Service Fee

An Expedited Fee applies when a CPA Verification Letter is requested on an accelerated timeline that falls outside Motta Financial’s standard turnaround period. This fee reflects:

  • Reprioritization of staff and CPA review time
  • Interruption of scheduled compliance and advisory work
  • Additional risk management and internal controls required for rushed deliverables

The expedited fee is in addition to any base fee for preparation of the CPA Verification Letter and must be paid prior to release of the final letter.
Service Terms:
• No Assurance or Attestation
    CPA Verification Letters are not audit, review, compilation, or assurance engagements. Motta Financial does not verify, audit, or independently validate the accuracy or completeness of information provided by the client. The letter is based solely on representations, records, and documents supplied by the client.
  • Information Dependency
    Expedited delivery timelines are contingent upon the client providing complete, accurate, and timely information. Delays or deficiencies in documentation may impact delivery timelines and do not constitute grounds for a fee reduction or refund.
  • Professional Judgment & Limitations
    Motta Financial reserves the right to:
    • Decline to issue a CPA Verification Letter if the requested language exceeds professional standards or creates undue reliance by a third party
    • Modify letter language to comply with AICPA ethical and professional requirements
    • Withdraw from the engagement if information provided appears misleading or inconsistent
  • No Third-Party Reliance
    CPA Verification Letters are prepared solely for the intended recipient identified in the letter. No other party may rely on the letter for decision-making purposes. Motta Financial assumes no responsibility to third parties beyond the named recipient.
  • Expedited Fee Is Non-Refundable
    Expedited fees compensate for priority handling and internal resource allocation. Once work has commenced, the expedited fee is non-refundable, regardless of outcome, third-party acceptance, or changes in client circumstances.
  • Payment Requirement
    All expedited fees must be paid in advance through Motta Financial’s secure payment system prior to release of the final letter.

Service Description:
Motta's Pre-409A Valuation Analysis service will provide an estimated valuation of your business to support planning, option grant strategy discussions, and to streamline a future “formal” 409A if/when needed. We will perform an investor graade business valuation to determine your company's fair market value. This includes analyzing financials, metrics, industry trends and outlook to reach a supportable value conclusion. The detailed valuation report will outline the methodologies used, assumptions made, sensitivity analysis, and value conclusion to help substantiate the valuation.

Important positioning (recommended language):
This engagement provides an indicative / planning valuation and supporting documentation. A company seeking 409A “safe harbor” protection generally relies on a valuation that meets the regulatory reasonableness framework and often uses an independent appraisal approach. Treasury regulations describe reasonable valuation factors and safe-harbor-style presumptions for private company equity valuations.

Valuation Approach (typical for Pre-409A)

  • Income Approach: DCF based on the 5-year forecast + terminal value assumptions
  • Market Approach: sanity checks using relevant multiples and/or guideline public comps (when data is available)
  • Adjustments / discounts: supportable discussion of marketability/illiquidity and company-specific risk (planning-grade; final 409A providers may apply more formal methodologies)

Deliverables — Pre-409A

  1. Pre-409A Valuation Memo including:
    • Purpose and valuation date
    • Standard of value (FMV framework discussion)
    • Methods used (DCF + market approach checks)
    • Key assumptions and sensitivities (WACC, terminal growth, margins, growth)
    • Valuation conclusion (enterprise and equity value; and, if requested, a preliminary common stock indication subject to cap table detail and preference terms)
  2. Assumptions & Inputs Appendix (written + tabular)
  3. Data Room Checklist / “409A Readiness” Packet (organizes the documents a formal 409A firm typically requests)
Service Terms:
1. Parties and Engagement

These Service Terms (“Terms”) govern Motta Financial’s provision of (i) a Five-Year Financial Statement Forecast and/or (ii) a Pre-409A Valuation Package (collectively, the “Services”).

2. Scope of Services

Motta will perform the Services described in the Service Description above, limited to the agreed deliverables. Any services not expressly included (e.g., audit, review, compilation, tax return preparation, legal services, fairness opinions, or a formal 409A safe-harbor appraisal) are excluded unless added by written change order.

3. Client Responsibilities

Client will:

  • Provide accurate, complete, and timely information and documents.
  • Make management available for interviews and review meetings.
  • Review drafts within agreed time windows and provide consolidated feedback.

Motta may rely on information provided by Client without independent verification, unless otherwise agreed.

4. Assumptions, Forward-Looking Information, and No Guarantee

Forecasts/projections are based on assumptions and forward-looking judgments. Actual results may differ—possibly materially—from projected results due to internal and external factors beyond Motta’s control.

5. Valuation-Specific Use and Reliance Limitations

  • The Pre-409A valuation is intended for planning and internal decision support unless expressly stated otherwise in the engagement letter.
  • If Client intends to use outputs to set equity compensation strike prices for compliance purposes, Client is responsible for ensuring the valuation approach and documentation meets applicable tax and regulatory requirements; Treasury regulations describe reasonable valuation considerations and presumptions for private stock valuations. Legal Information Institute+2IRS+2
  • No third party may rely on Motta’s work without Motta’s prior written consent.

6. Deliverable Format and Acceptance

Deliverables may include Excel workbooks, PDFs, and written memos/appendices. Deliverables are deemed accepted when delivered unless Client provides written notice of material nonconformity within 10 business days.

7. Revisions and Change Orders

Fees include up to:

  • Two (2) consolidated revision rounds on the forecast model after initial draft delivery, and
  • One (1) consolidated revision round on the Pre-409A memo after draft delivery.

Additional revisions, new scenarios, structural changes, or scope expansion are billed via change order.

8. Fees, Billing, and Payment

  • Fees are fixed-fee or fixed-fee + hourly ($175 - $450 per hour).
  • Typical payment schedule (customize per engagement):
    • 50% deposit at kickoff
    • 40% upon delivery of draft forecast + draft pre-409A memo
    • 10% upon delivery of final package
  • Past-due amounts may pause work until current.

9. Confidentiality and Data Security

Motta will maintain confidentiality of Client materials and use secure exchange methods for document transfer. Client authorizes Motta to use necessary subcontractors or platforms under confidentiality obligations.

10. Workpapers and Intellectual Property

Motta retains ownership of templates, tools, and workpapers. Client receives a non-transferable license to use final deliverables internally for the intended purpose.

11. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Motta’s aggregate liability related to the Services is limited to the fees paid for the specific Services giving rise to the claim. Motta will not be liable for consequential, incidental, or punitive damages.

12. Termination

Either party may terminate with written notice. Client remains responsible for fees incurred through termination date, including non-cancelable third-party costs (if any).

13. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law

Add your preferred venue/state governing law language (e.g., Nevada/Massachusetts—choose consistent with the client contract).

Service Description:
Service Description

Our Monthly Bookkeeping for Exempt Organizations service ensures accurate, compliant financial records for nonprofits, charities, and 501(c)(3) entities. We focus on nonprofit-specific fund accounting and reporting to give your leadership team, board, funders, and auditors confidence in your financial data.

Core Deliverables

  • Monthly bank and credit card reconciliation
  • Transaction categorization using nonprofit fund accounting
  • Tracking of income (donations, grants, program revenue) and expenses
  • Recording restricted vs. unrestricted funds
  • Basic financial statements (Balance Sheet, Statement of Activities)
  • Accounts payable and receivable support
  • Monthly closing and quality review
  • Cloud-based accounting system setup/maintenance (e.g., QuickBooks Online Nonprofit)

Benefits

  • Clean, up-to-date books for board and stakeholder transparency
  • Compliance with nonprofit accounting standards
  • Predictable monthly reporting and financial oversight
Service Terms:
Engagement:
This bookkeeping engagement begins upon signed agreement and continues on a month-to-month basis until terminated per the Cancellation Terms below.

Deliverables:
Monthly financial records, reconciliations, and reports are delivered no later than the 15th business day of the following month, assuming timely access to documents.

Client Responsibilities:
Client must provide required source documents (bank statements, receipts, uploaded transactions) by the 5th of each month. Delays may impact reporting timelines and could incur additional fees.

Billing and Payment:
Fees are billed monthly in advance and payable within 15 days of invoice. Late payments may accrue interest and pause services until resolved.

Confidentiality:
All financial data and internal records are held in strict confidence and used solely for the provision of agreed services.

Termination:
Either party may terminate with 30 days written notice. Outstanding fees through the termination date remain due and payable.

Either party may terminate with 60 days written notice for this higher-tier engagement due to the onboarding and recurring commitment.

Service Description:
Motta Financial will prepare the U.S. Gift (and Generation-Skipping Transfer) Tax Return (IRS Form 709) based on information and supporting documentation provided by the client. Services include:

  • Gathering and organizing gift details (recipient, date, property type, and value)
  • Determining appropriate reporting treatment for annual exclusion gifts and taxable gifts
  • Preparing Form 709 and required schedules (including gift-splitting election and GST allocations, if applicable)
  • Providing a draft for client review and addressing reasonable questions and corrections prior to filing
  • Delivering filing instructions and/or submitting the return via the applicable filing method (e-file where available or paper filing where required)
  • Providing a final copy of the filed return for the client’s records

Optional add-ons (if you want in Ignition as selectable line items):

  • Prior-year gift reconstruction and cumulative tracking (if gifts occurred in prior years and returns were not filed)
  • Coordination with valuation professionals and review of valuation report for tax reporting consistency (prep-side coordination only)
  • Amended Form 709
  • IRS notice response related to the filed Form 709 (separately scoped)
Service Terms:
Client Responsibilities

  • Client will provide complete and accurate information regarding gifts made during the tax year, including dates, recipients, descriptions, and values.
  • Client will provide supporting documentation for each gift (bank/brokerage confirmations, transfer documents, deeds, entity documents, trust documents, etc.).
  • If a gift requires a valuation or appraisal, client is responsible for engaging and paying the valuation professional unless otherwise agreed in writing.

Valuations / Appraisals

  • Motta Financial does not provide appraisal or valuation services as part of this engagement unless expressly stated in the scope.
  • Motta Financial will report values provided by the client and/or the client’s valuation professional and may request additional support if values appear inconsistent with documentation or customary reporting practices.

Scope Limitations

  • This engagement is limited to preparation of IRS Form 709 for the specified tax year and does not include broader estate planning legal advice.
  • Legal services (trust drafting, deed preparation, entity restructuring, etc.) are outside the scope. We will coordinate with the client’s attorney upon request.

Timing / Deadlines

  • Completion and timely filing depend on receipt of all required information and documents.
  • If information is received late, Motta Financial may recommend filing an extension (where applicable) or may not be able to meet the filing deadline.

Client Review & Approval

  • Client is responsible for reviewing the draft return and confirming accuracy prior to signing/authorizing filing.
  • Client will sign all required authorization/signature forms prior to submission.

Fees & Out-of-Scope Work

  • The quoted fee covers preparation and filing of the Form 709 as described above.
  • Additional work requested or required due to missing/incomplete records, prior-year reconstruction, valuation coordination beyond basic reporting support, amended returns, or IRS correspondence will be billed as an additional fixed fee or hourly charge per a separate written agreement/change order.

Record Retention

  • Client should retain the filed Form 709 permanently, as future gift/estate reporting may rely on cumulative lifetime tracking.

Service Description:
Motta Financial provides sell-side mergers and acquisitions accounting support to help business owners prepare for a transaction, respond efficiently during diligence, and support accounting-related transition matters through close and post-close handoff.

Our role is to serve as your accounting and finance support partner throughout the transaction process, particularly where Motta has historically managed bookkeeping, controllership, outsourced accounting, or fractional CFO functions. We work alongside ownership, legal counsel, investment bankers/brokers, internal finance teams, and the buyer’s diligence team to help organize financial information, address accounting questions, and reduce execution friction.

Services may include

  • Financial readiness support prior to market
    • Review of historical financial statements and underlying books
    • Identification of accounting clean-up items and normalization considerations
    • Assistance preparing transaction-ready financial schedules and support files
    • Coordination of recurring reporting packages used in buyer review
  • Sell-side diligence support
    • Preparation and organization of accounting schedules and supporting documentation
    • Responses to buyer, QoE, and diligence requests related to accounting records
    • Analysis of revenue, margins, payroll, working capital, debt, accruals, and other key balances
    • Support for management explanations of historical results, trends, and accounting policies
    • Coordination with third-party diligence providers, bankers, and counsel as needed
  • Technical accounting and transaction support
    • Assistance with accounting memos, policy support, and transaction-related accounting analyses
    • Support for carve-out, accrual, cutoff, revenue recognition, and balance sheet matters
    • Assistance interpreting buyer accounting requests and translating them into actionable deliverables
    • Support related to GAAP / accrual-basis, PCAOB-oriented, or public-company style reporting expectations, depending on engagement tier
  • Purchase accounting and transition support
    • Coordination with buyer and/or buyer advisors on accounting items historically managed by Motta
    • Transition support for close process, trial balance mapping, schedules, reconciliations, and historical accounting support
    • Assistance with purchase accounting inputs and documentation support, as requested
    • Post-close handoff support for finance and accounting knowledge transfer
    • Limited post-close support for accounting continuity, open items, and transition questions

Business Transformation

Foundation: Clean Up & Stabilization

This engagement is designed to establish accurate and reliable financial data as the foundation for business decision-making.

Motta Financial will perform a structured cleanup and normalization of your accounting records, ensuring that your financial statements are complete, consistent, and aligned with standard accounting practices.

Scope of Services:

  • Historical financial cleanup and reclassification
  • Chart of Accounts redesign and standardization
  • Balance sheet review and reconciliation support
  • Identification and correction of material errors
  • Establishment of baseline accounting policies
  • Delivery of normalized financial statements

Key Outcomes:

  • Clean, reliable financials
  • Consistent categorization and reporting structure
  • Improved confidence in financial data

This service focuses on building a solid financial foundation and does not include ongoing reporting, forecasting, or advisory services.
Service Terms:
Service Terms

  • Engagement Type: Fixed-fee project
  • Typical Timeline: 3–6 weeks depending on data quality

Scope Limitations:

  • Limited to agreed historical periods (typically 12–24 months)
  • Does not include ongoing bookkeeping, reporting, or FP&A
  • Does not include tax preparation or filings

Client Responsibilities:

  • Provide full access to accounting systems and records
  • Respond to requests within 2–3 business days
  • Confirm final classifications where judgment is required

Data Reliance:
We rely on client-provided data and do not perform audit-level verification.

Change Orders:
Significant data gaps, missing records, or additional entities will require a revised scope and fee.

Deliverables:

  • Cleaned financial statements
  • Updated Chart of Accounts

Payment Terms:
Due upfront prior to commencement.

Limitation:
No guarantee of financial outcomes; this engagement is limited to data cleanup and organization.
