Service Description:
Overview
 

This service is designed for clients who have operated a business without maintaining formal accounting records during the tax year. Motta Financial will reconstruct the financial activity using available documentation (bank statements, credit card statements, receipts, and other supporting records) in order to prepare accurate financials and complete the business tax return.

Scope of Services

We will:

  1. Data Collection & Organization
    • Gather all available financial records, including:
      • Bank statements
      • Credit card statements
      • Loan statements (if applicable)
      • Receipts and invoices
    • Organize documents into a usable format for reconstruction
  2. Transaction Reconstruction
    • Rebuild income and expense activity from source documents
    • Categorize transactions into appropriate tax classifications
    • Identify missing or unclear transactions requiring client clarification
  3. Financial Statement Preparation
    • Prepare a reconstructed Profit & Loss statement
    • Identify potential balance sheet items (loans, assets, etc.) if applicable
  4. Tax-Ready Adjustments
    • Apply reasonable assumptions where documentation is incomplete (with client approval)
    • Flag non-deductible or questionable items
    • Prepare records suitable for tax return preparation
  5. Coordination with Tax Preparation
    • Deliver finalized numbers for inclusion in the business tax return

OUT OF SCOPE: 

  • Ongoing bookkeeping or monthly accounting
  • Audit-level substantiation or forensic accounting
  • Reconstruction of missing records not provided by the client
  • Sales tax filings or payroll reconstruction (unless separately engaged)
  • Guaranteed audit defense for reconstructed records
Service Terms:
1. Client Responsibilities

  • Provide complete and timely access to all financial records
  • Respond to clarification requests within 5 business days
  • Ensure all income sources are disclosed
  • Maintain original documentation for audit purposes

2. Data Limitations & Assumptions

Due to incomplete records:

  • Financials are prepared using best available information
  • Certain transactions may be estimated or categorized based on reasonable assumptions
  • Final results are dependent on the quality and completeness of records provided

3. Turnaround Time

  • Estimated completion: 3–8 weeks, depending on:
    • Volume of transactions
    • Quality of documentation
    • Client responsiveness

4. Additional Work / Scope Creep

The following will trigger additional fees:

  • Additional accounts or entities discovered after engagement begins
  • Excessive missing documentation requiring reconstruction beyond standard scope
  • Significant rework due to late or revised information

5. Audit & Compliance Disclaimer

  • Reconstructed books may carry higher audit risk
  • Client is responsible for maintaining supporting documentation
  • Motta Financial does not guarantee audit outcomes

6. Payment Terms (Ignition Standard)

  • Engagement requires upfront payment or deposit
  • Work may be paused if invoices are unpaid
  • Fees are based on estimated scope and may adjust if scope materially changes

Service Description:
This service is designed for straightforward IRS notices that require clarification or basic documentation.

Motta Financial will:

  • Review the IRS notice and identify the specific issue(s)
  • Provide a document request checklist tailored to the notice
  • Review supporting documentation provided by the client
  • Prepare and submit a formal written response to the IRS
  • Provide guidance on next steps, if applicable

This service is appropriate for single-issue notices with minimal complexity and no prior audit history impacting the position.
Service Terms:
• This service includes one (1) response to the IRS notice.
  • Additional IRS correspondence, expanded scope, or new issues identified will require a separate engagement or upgrade to a higher service tier.
  • Client is responsible for providing complete and accurate documentation to support all items reported on the tax return.
  • Motta Financial does not audit or independently verify information provided but will rely on documentation submitted.
  • Turnaround time is dependent on timely receipt of requested documents.
  • Representation does not include IRS phone calls, appeals, amended returns, or legal representation.
  • If documentation is insufficient to support the tax position, Motta Financial will advise on alternative strategies, which may require additional services.
